data.title: Cryptos like Bitcoin & Ethereum slide, need close above $117,000. Bitcoin at $109,036.62, Ethereum $3,940.21. Liquidations hit $737 million in last 24 hours. Traders uncertain.
data.description: Leading cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin and Ethereum, experienced declines on Thursday, with Bitcoin dropping to $107,500, its lowest in over three months. Ethereum hit an intraday low of $3,829.65 before recovering to $3,940. Selling pressure was evident as Bitcoin and Ethereum's trading volumes dropped 22% and 5% respectively in the last 24 hours. Liquidations amounted to $737 million, wiping out over $530 million in bullish long positions. Despite the downturn, Bitcoin's open interest rose by 2.85% to $73 billion, suggesting traders may be opening new short positions. Gold, in contrast, reached new highs. The full article is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Fed's Waller suggests 5 more rate cuts needed to reach neutral policy stance, citing disinflation and labor softness.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller suggested the central bank may need to reduce rates by up to 125 basis points to achieve a more neutral policy stance due to signs of disinflation and weakening labor demand. Waller mentioned during a Bloomberg Council on Foreign Relations event that approximately five additional rate cuts could be necessary to reach the neutral rate, a level where the economy operates at full employment and stable inflation. Waller referred to the Summary of Economic Projections, indicating that the median rate is around 3%, leaving a gap of 125 basis points for the committee to reach neutrality if economic conditions move closer to the target. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Bitfarms upsizes to US$500 million in convertible notes offering, with an option to purchase an additional $88 million.
data.description: Bitfarms Ltd., a North American company focusing on energy and digital infrastructure, has revealed the pricing of its offering of US$500 million aggregate principal amount of 1.375% convertible senior notes due 2031. The company granted initial purchasers of the Convertible Notes an option to acquire up to an additional US$88 million aggregate principal amount of the notes, within a 13-day window. Initially intended as a $300 million issuance (or $360 million if the initial purchasers fully exercise their option), the total offering size was raised. The offering, expected to close around October 21, 2025, is subject to customary closing conditions, including TSX approval.

data.title: Former PayPal and Intuit CEO Bill Harris to present Evergreen Wealth & Tax Strategies for Options, RSUs, and QSBS in Silicon Valley.
data.description: In a recent announcement, it was shared that Bill Harris, who previously served as the CEO of PayPal and Intuit, will be presenting strategies focused on wealth preservation and tax planning. The event, scheduled to take place in Silicon Valley, will specifically focus on options, RSUs (Restricted Stock Units), and QSBS (Qualified Small Business Stock). Attendees will have the opportunity to gain insights from Harris on maximizing their wealth through these investment avenues. This showcase is poised to offer valuable information to individuals looking to enhance their financial prospects.

data.title: Shandong's Linyi aims to become a global logistics and trade hub as 2,000 buyers from 99 countries attend conference.
data.description: On October 10, the 3rd Linyi Trade City International Sourcing Conference for Belt and Road Cooperation commenced at Linyi Mall in China's Shandong province. The event saw participation from more than 2,000 international buyers representing 99 countries and regions.

data.title: Colabor Group Inc. (TSX: GCL) announced third quarter results on Sept 6, 2025, in Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville, Quebec.
data.description: In Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville, Quebec, on October 16, 2025, Colabor Group Inc. (TSX: GCL) released its financial performance for the third quarter concluded on September 6, 2025.

data.title: Bank First Corporation (Nasdaq: BFC) gets regulatory approval to acquire Centre 1 Bancorp, Inc. in Manitowoc, Wisconsin on Oct. 16, 2025.
data.description: In Manitowoc, Wisconsin, on October 16, 2025, Bank First Corporation (Nasdaq: BFC) revealed that it has obtained the necessary regulatory green lights to finalize its merger with Centre 1 Bancorp, Inc. This move involves Bank First, N.A. and marks a significant step in the process. The merger, once completed, will bring together the two entities and create a stronger financial institution with a notable impact in the banking sector. This development opens up opportunities for growth and expansion for Bank First Corporation.

data.title: Sagora Senior Living acquires Wilshire Senior Living in Burleson, Texas, expanding its portfolio of senior living communities.
data.description: In Burleson, Texas on October 16, 2025, Sagora Senior Living revealed the inclusion of Wilshire Senior Living to its expanding network of senior living communities nationwide. This latest addition marks the ownership of Wilshire Senior Living by Sagora Senior Living, bolstering their portfolio with a notable facility in Burleson.
